title: 奉命谋杀
summary:
Leo Aigle reflects on his history with his late wife, Rachel, and her ambitious attempt to construct an artificial family through the adoption of several children: Mary, Mickey, Tina, Hester, and Jackie. While Leo supported the idea of providing homes, he felt these children lacked the ancestral blood, ambition, and integrity of his own lineage. He notes that Jackie, in particular, grew into a manipulative juvenile delinquent. Despite the efforts of both Leo and Rachel to support him, Jackie’s academic failures and criminal tendencies transformed him into an angry, threatening man who demanded money from them. Following Rachel's death, Leo found emotional companionship in his loyal secretary, Gwendolyn, though he was unable to marry her during Rachel's lifetime.

In a separate setting, Donald Craig visits Dr. McMaster to discuss his distress regarding his relationship with Hester Aigle. Donald expresses his love for Hester but is troubled by recent developments concerning "justice for Jackie Aigle." He suggests that the situation might have been better if certain new evidence had not surfaced. Dr. McMaster observes that many others, including the police chief and a man recently returned from the South Pole, share this sentiment. Donald reveals that on the night Mr. Calgary visited the Aigle house, Hester called him in a distraught state, canceling their plans to attend a lecture in Dreyfus because she had heard unsettling news.
